Troubleshooting

Public

Troubleshooting 1186

This section provides troubleshooting commands related to this user guide. For more troubleshooting
commands, please the refer to the AOS-CX Command-Line Interface Guide.

Subtopics

troubleshoot l3

troubleshoot l3

Syntax

troubleshoot l3

   advance [parameters <parameters>]

   arp [{advance [parameters <parameters>]}|basic|verbose]

   basic [parameters <parameters>]

   bfd [basic|verbose]

   bgp [basic|verbose]

   config [basic|verbose]

   evpn [basic|verbose]

   ip-apps [basic|verbose]

   ospf [advance|basic|config|verbose]

   ospfv3 [advance|basic|verbose]

   rip [basic|verbose]

   static-route [basic|verbose]

   vrrp [basic|verbose]

Description

Troubleshoot issues with Layer-3 features and protocols,.

Parameter

advance

arp

Description

Display advanced troubleshooting. The troubleshoot l3 advan
ce command displays advanced troubleshooting information for
all L3 features. Include the advance parameter after a specific L
3 feature name to display advanced troubleshooting informatio
n for that feature only.

Troubleshoot the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP). The tro
ubleshooting l3 arp command supports the additional param
eters "<parameters>" option to display output for the following
parameter options:

•  "neighbor <ip_address>": Troubleshoot issues with the spe

cified neighbor.

Public

troubleshoot l3 1187

Parameter

Description

•  "neighbor <ip_address> interface <interface>": Troublesh

oot all neighbors' issues on a specific interface.

•  "neighbor <ip_address> interface <interface> vrf <vrf‐n
ame>": Troubleshoot all neighbors' issues on a specific inter
face in a VRF.

•  "neighbor6 <ip_address>": Troubleshoot issues with the sp

ecified ipv6 neighbor.

•  "neighbor6 <ip_address> interface <interface>": Troubles

hoot all ipv6 neighbors' issues on a specific interface.
•  "neighbor6 <ip_address> interface <interface> vrf <vrf

‐name>": Troubleshoot all ipv6 neighbors' issues on a spe
cific interface in a VRF.

•  "tx_rx <interface>": Troubleshoot TX/RX packet statistics

for ARP traffic on a specific interface

•  "tx_rx ": Troubleshoot TX/RX packet statistics for ARP traff

ic on all interfaces

•  "evpn_vxlan <ip_address>": Troubleshoot EVPN VxLAN ne

ighbor entries and their status.

•  "evpn_vxlan": Troubleshoot all EVPN VxLAN neighbor entr

ies and their status.

•  "route_resolution <ip_address>": Troubleshoot the route
resolution table and validates the L2 details configuration
for a specific neighbor.

•  "route_resolution": Troubleshoot the route resolution table

and validates L2 details configuration.

•  "l2cg_te <ip_address>": Troubleshoot the relationship betw
een L2 composite groups, tunnel endpoints, and a neighbor
table entry for specific neighbor.

•  "l2cg_te":Troubleshoot the relationship between L2 compo
site groups, tunnel endpoints, and a neighbor table entry fo
r all neighbors.

NOTE
that the "<parameters>" string must be inclose
d in double quotation marks (").

basic

Display basic information for l3 errors on the network, includin
g the error message, message ID, severity level, and when the m
essage was last reported. The troubleshoot l3 basic command
displays basic troubleshooting information for all L3 features. In

Public

troubleshoot l3 1188

Parameter

Description

bfd

bgp

config

evpn

ip‐apps

ospf

ospfv3

clude the basic parameter after a specific L3 feature name to di
splay basic troubleshooting information for that feature only.

Troubleshoot Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD).

Troubleshoot the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP).

Troubleshoot configuration issues impacting L3 features.

Troubleshoot Ethernet VPN (EVPN).

Troubleshoot IP applications.

Troubleshoot Open Shortest Path First version 2 (OSPF). The t
roubleshooting l3 ospf command supports the additional para
meters "<parameters>" option to display output for the followi
ng parameter options:

•

•

•

•

•

•

•

"neighbor <router‐id>"`: Troubleshoot issues with the spe
cified neighbor.
"neighbor <router‐id> interface <interface>": Troublesh
oot neighbor issues on a specific interface.
"neighbor <router‐id> interface <interface> vrf <vrf‐na
me>": Troubleshoot neighbor issues on a specific interface i
n a VRF.
"interface <interface>": Troubleshoot issues with the specifi
ed interface.
"interface <interface> vrf <vrf‐name>": Troubleshoot inter
face issues in a specific VRF.
"route <ipv6‐prefix>": Troubleshoot issues with the specifi
ed IPv6 route.
"interface vlan{vlan_id}": Troubleshoot issues on a specific V
LAN.

NOTE
that the "<parameters>" string must be inclose
d in double quotation marks (").

Troubleshoot Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3). Th
e troubleshooting l3 ospfv3 command supports the additional
parameters "<parameters>" option to display output for the fol
lowing parameter options:

•  parameters "neighbor <router‐id>"`: Troubleshoot issues

with the specified neighbor.

Public

troubleshoot l3 1189

Parameter

Description

•  parameters "neighbor <router‐id> interface <interface>":

Troubleshoot neighbor issues on a specific interface.

•  parameters "neighbor <router‐id> interface <interface> vr
f <vrf‐name>": Troubleshoot neighbor issues on a specific
interface in a VRF.

•  parameters "interface <interface>": Troubleshoot issues wit

h the specified interface.

•  parameters "interface <interface> vrf <vrf‐name>": Troubl

eshoot interface issues in a specific VRF.

•  parameters "route <ipv6‐prefix>": Troubleshoot issues wit

h the specified IPv6 route.

•  parameters "interface vlan{vlan_id}": Troubleshoot issues on

a specific VLAN.

Troubleshoot the Routing Information Protocol (RIP)

Troubleshoot Static Route Configurations

Troubleshoot the Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP).

rip

static‐route

vrrp

Examples

Performing advanced troubleshooting for OSPFv3 neighbor issues on a specific interface:

switch# troubleshoot l3 ospfv3 advance parameters "neighbor 1.1.1.1

interface 1/1/1"

--------------------------------------------------

Advance troubleshoot

--------------------------------------------------

ospfv3 : error: RCVD_BAD_UNKNOWN.1/1/1

         Possible errors:
         1. DUT and Peer Not in Same Network

         error: RCVD_DEAD_MISMATCH1/1/1

         error: 1/1/1 TX_RX_MISMATCH

         error: Neighbor not found. Troubleshoot interface for any issues

with the ospfv3 interface.

====================================================

Troubleshoot OSPFv3 executed successfully
Displaying TX/RX packet statistics for ARP traffic on a specific interface to identify potential packet loss or
asymmetric traffic patterns:

troubleshoot l3 arp advance parameters "tx_rx vlan100"

--------------------------------------------------

Public

troubleshoot l3 1190

Detailed troubleshoot outcome

--------------------------------------------------

arp  : error: Existence of Loop on the Port: vlan100 in the Topology

       error: Packets dropped: 14613849

====================================================

Troubleshoot ARP executed successfully

mber)

Accessing Updates

You can access updates from the HPE Aruba Networking Support Portal at https://
networkingsupport.hpe.com.

Some software products provide a mechanism for accessing software updates through the product interface.
Review your product documentation to identify the recommended software update method.

To subscribe to eNewsletters and alerts:

https://networkingsupport.hpe./notifications/subscriptions (requires an active HPE Aruba Networking
Support Portal account to manage subscriptions). Security notices are viewable without an HPE Aruba
Networking Support Portal account.

Warranty Information

To view warranty information for your product, go to https://www.arubanetworks.com/support-services/
product-warranties/.

Regulatory Information

Public

Accessing Updates 1193

To view the regulatory information for your product, view the Safety and Compliance Information for
Server, Storage, Power, Networking, and Rack Products, available at https://www.hpe.com/support/Safety-
Compliance-EnterpriseProducts

Additional regulatory information

HPE Aruba Networking is committed to providing our customers with information about the chemical
substances in our products as needed to comply with legal requirements, environmental data (company
programs, product recycling, energy efficiency), and safety information and compliance data, (RoHS and
WEEE). For more information, see https://www.arubanetworks.com/company/about-us/environmental-
citizenship/.

Documentation Feedback

HPE Aruba Networking is committed to providing documentation that meets your needs. To help us improve
the documentation, send any errors, suggestions, or comments to Documentation Feedback (docsfeedback-
switching@hpe.com). When submitting your feedback, include the document title, part number, edition, and
publication date located on the front cover of the document. For online help content, include the product
name, product version, help edition, and publication date located on the legal notices page.

Public

Documentation Feedback 1194
